entities.name: BIURET
entities.description: A chemical impurity found in urea fertilizer that affects nitrogen transformation in soil

entities.name: UREA NITROGEN
entities.description: The nitrogen component of urea that undergoes various transformations in the soil

entities.name: SANDY LOAM
entities.description: A type of soil with a pH of 7.7 used in the study

entities.name: UREA HYDROLYSIS
entities.description: The process of urea breaking down into ammonia and carbon dioxide

entities.name: NH+4
entities.description: Ammonium, a form of nitrogen resulting from urea hydrolysis

entities.name: NO-2
entities.description: Nitrite, an intermediate form of nitrogen in the nitrification process

entities.name: NO-3
entities.description: Nitrate, the final oxidized form of nitrogen in the nitrification process

entities.name: NITRITE TOXICITY
entities.description: The toxic effect caused by the accumulation of nitrite in the soil

entities.name: UREA FERTILIZER
entities.description: A nitrogen-rich fertilizer that may contain biuret as an impurity

entities.name: PHILIPPINES
entities.description: The country where the International Rice Research Institute is located

entities.name: INTERNATIONAL RICE RESEARCH INSTITUTE
entities.description: The research institution where the author is currently based

relationships.description: inhibits the conversion of NH+4 to NO-2
inhibits the oxidation of NO-2 to NO-3
